interaction.prompt: 4-on-4 OT. Their forward in the slot is the most dangerous. Who should YOU (the C) be assigned to?

feedback.right: Slot threat. C in 4-on-4 always picks up the most dangerous player; LD covers the wall, RD reads off you.
feedback.wrong: On the wall is the LD's job; the point is too far. Center owns the slot — that's where goals come from.

tip: 4-on-4 DZ: C picks up the highest-danger player (usually slot). Defenders cover the rest in support.
why: Open-ice 4-on-4 can break a coverage if everyone runs to the puck. The C's job is to nail down the most dangerous attacker so the rest can read off.
